headline: US Stock Market Today: S&P 500 Futures Edge Higher As Inflation Pressure Eases
summary: The Morning Bull - US Market Morning Update Tuesday, Jul, 21 2026 US stock futures are pointing slightly higher this morning, as investors weigh softer US inflation against rising global interest rate worries. The US 10 year Treasury yield has eased to about 4.52% after cooler price data, which helps reduce pressure on borrowing costs for households and companies. At the same time, import prices rose 0.3% in June instead of falling, a reminder that the cost of goods coming into the country is...

headline: Global Payments (GPN) Stock Looks Stretched After A 55% Five Year Fall
summary: Global Payments stock has rebounded in the short term, yet after a 54.8% decline over the last five years and a low overall valuation score, the recent share price around US$82.37 still screens as expensive rather than a clear bargain. Over the last five years, Global Payments shareholders have seen the stock fall 54.8%, which frames the recent bounce as a recovery attempt rather than a long term win. Expectations for the company to keep converting payment volumes into steady earnings can...

headline: Does Morgan Stanley’s GPN Upgrade Reveal a Deeper Shift in Global Payments’ Core Platform Narrative?
summary: Morgan Stanley recently upgraded Global Payments to Overweight, citing improving execution, stronger share repurchase capacity, and constructive feedback on its Genius and Worldpay payment platforms. The brokerage’s focus on Global Payments’ competitive positioning in integrated small-business solutions and enterprise processing highlights how product traction is shaping expectations for the company’s outlook. We’ll now examine how Morgan Stanley’s increased confidence in Global Payments’...

headline: Global Payments (GPN) Could Be 11% Undervalued On Morgan Stanley Upgrade
summary: Morgan Stanley’s upgrade of Global Payments (GPN) to Overweight has put the stock back in focus, as the broker pointed to execution, product feedback and capital returns as key positives. See our latest analysis for Global Payments. The recent Morgan Stanley upgrade coincides with a strong near term run, with Global Payments showing a 23.16% 1 month share price return and a 9.06% year to date share price return. However, the 5 year total shareholder return is down 54.75%, suggesting current...
